title: calendula sancta subsp. cristagalli
intro: Calendula sancta subsp. cristagalli, commonly known as pot marigold, is a plant species in the daisy family (Asteraceae) that is native to the Mediterranean region. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help to soothe skin irritations, wounds, and infections. The therapeutic actions of calendula sancta subsp. cristagalli include wound healing, skin protection, and anti-inflammatory responses, attributed to its bioactive constituents such as flavonoids, carotenoids, and saponins. These bioactive compounds contribute to the plant's therapeutic properties, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies and skincare products.
benefits: Calendula sancta subsp. cristagalli has many benefits, such as promoting skin health through its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help soothe burns, wounds, and eczema, and even aid in the reduction of acne and rosacea. The plant's medicinal properties also extend to its ability to alleviate digestive issues like constipation and diarrhea, as well as reduce symptoms of colds and flu. Additionally, calendula's antiseptic and antibacterial properties can help prevent infection and promote wound healing, making it a valuable remedy for surgical incisions and injuries. By harnessing the medicinal properties of calendula sancta subsp. cristagalli, individuals can improve their overall health and well-being, leading to a more active and vibrant life.
actions: Calendula sancta subsp. cristagalli has many therapeutic actions, such as anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties, which help to soothe and heal various skin conditions, including wounds, burns, and eczema. These therapeutic actions also exhibit antioxidant and antihistamine properties, making them effective in reducing inflammation, itching, and redness associated with conditions like rosacea, acne, and hives. Furthermore, the plant's antiseptic and antimicrobial properties make it an effective remedy for minor cuts and scrapes, promoting faster healing and reducing the risk of infection, thereby improving overall hygiene and health. By harnessing the therapeutic actions of Calendula sancta subsp. cristagalli, individuals can experience improved wound healing, reduced inflammation, and enhanced overall well-being.
constituents: Calendula sancta subsp. cristagalli has many active constituents, such as flavonoids like quercetin and kaempferol, carotenoids like beta-carotene and lutein, phenolic acids like caffeic acid and ferulic acid, and terpenoids like calendulol and isocalendulol, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The flavonoids and phenolic acids in calendula have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, helping to soothe skin irritations and wounds, while the carotenoids and terpenoids may improve eye health and reduce the risk of age-related macular degeneration. The medicinal constituents of calendula also have antioxidant properties, which can protect against cell damage and reduce the risk of chronic diseases like heart disease and cancer. By consuming calendula or applying its extracts topically, individuals can potentially improve their overall health and well-being, and enjoy a better quality of life.
parts: Calendula sancta subsp. cristagalli has many medicinal parts, such as the flowers, leaves, and roots, which are used in traditional medicine. The flowers contain flavonoids like quercetin and kaempferol, as well as triterpenoid saponins, which have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The leaves of the plant are rich in essential oils, including calendula oil, which contains sesquiterpenes like alpha-bisabolol, and terpenoids like beta-caryophyllene, which exhibit anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The roots of the plant are also used in herbal medicine, and are said to contain sesquiterpenes, triterpenoids, and phenolic compounds, which may contribute to their antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties.
preparations: Calendula sancta subsp. cristagalli has many herbal preparations, such as teas, tinctures, salves, and infusions, that are used for various medicinal purposes. Calendula tea, for example, is used to treat digestive issues and as a mild laxative, while calendula tincture is used to soothe wounds and reduce inflammation. Calendula salves, made by infusing the plant in a carrier oil, are used to treat skin conditions such as eczema and acne, and to promote wound healing. Additionally, calendula infusions, often used in skincare products, are valued for their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making them effective in treating skin irritations and promoting skin health.
side_effects: Calendula sancta subsp. cristagalli can have side effects if used improperly, such as skin irritation, allergic reactions, and increased sensitivity to sunlight. Prolonged or excessive use can lead to skin conditions like eczema and dermatitis. Ingesting calendula may cause gastrointestinal issues, including nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea. Additionally, using calendula as a treatment for internal health issues without proper medical guidance can interact with other medications and exacerbate existing health conditions.
